## [00:00 - 02:30]

When I meet with strangers, I find it really difficult to come up with questions to ask them. Well, that to me really is just a question of preparation. Instead of leaving it to chance and going, when I meet someone for the first time, I hope that in that moment I'm going to be able to come up with questions to ask them. Have a list of five questions that you keep in your back pocket. Be prepared. They say that luck is defined by when preparation meets opportunity. So instead of just hoping you're going to be lucky when you meet someone and you come up with the right questions, prepare to be lucky every time you meet someone, where you're able to just have the questions in your back pocket and you know them at a moment's notice. So I can share with you some of mine and depending on your comfort level in social situations, your list of questions might be different and it's okay. The first one, super easy. Everyone does it. Hey, how are you going? You know that by asking that, most likely the person is just going to say, yeah, yeah, good. I'm doing well. How are you doing? You're going to have a plain response.
